Speaker 1: This next tool will be the quickest and easiest one, but it's so important. Here's what I
know about masterminds, and you'll see this. Even if you do it virtual or live, or you're
doing it for somebody else, like we say, you're going to want people to introduce
themselves. I have to tell you, when I first started doing masterminds, wow, over
probably 15 years ago I used to say, "Hey, could you stand up real quick and introduce
yourself?" Some people would stand up and say, "Hey, I'm Bob. I do this for a living. This
is a great event, and I'm so happy to be here." Someone else would stand up and tell
their whole life story, and then they never knew where to cut them off. I was like, "Uh,
uh, uh." Some people would take 20 minutes, and the last thing you want to do is have a
mastermind, and get people to stand up, and go around the room, and it take an hour to
just tell who they are and what they do.
Taking this from a mastermind I was in and the genius network mastermind and
tweaking it for mine years ago, I want to show you the who am I and what do I do tool?
This is where people stand up to introduce themselves in just a minute or two, and it's
consistent for everyone because you tell them they can't write more than the lines that
are on the page. You could click below. You can see this tool. You'll see the example of
mine. I'm going to read it. It's basically, I'm blank. I love to blank. The best thing about
my business is, and I'm part of this mastermind because. In just a moment, it gives you a
highlight of who the person is. You have an example on this page, but it's I'm Dean
Graziosi.
Imagine if I stood up, I was at your mastermind, and I was learning. It was a marketing
mastermind. I'd say, "I'm Dean Graziosi. I love to inspire people to take action on things
that can massively improve their lives." The best thing about my business is watching
the results that people get with my education and my training. I'm a part of this
mastermind because I want even better skills to attract the perfect client. Look at that.
That was all in minutes. I promise you, for me, the previous tool I did, the where are you
tool, the clarity equals bigger future tool, I like starting with that sometimes before I
even do the intro, so people are in it. They're emotionally connected, and now you go
around the room. You can intro people.
You could to it at any time you want. You could do it at the end of day one. You could do
it as the first thing you do, but this is how you keep people clear and concise. As simple
as this sounds, it took me 20 masterminds before I got this dialed in to make an impact,
share who they are, and then sit your butt down, and let's get to training.
